mechanism_narrative: A 5% increase in real GDP over the past quarter signals a strengthening demand environment that supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods of real GDP growth < 3% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 10% increase in industrial production over the past month signals a strengthening supply environment that supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods of industrial production growth < 5% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 50-basis-point decline in the federal funds rate over the past 3 months signals a easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% increase in the unemployment rate over the past month signals a weakening labor market that supports negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods of unemployment rate decline > 0.2% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 10% increase in housing starts over the past quarter signals a strengthening demand environment that supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days, provided the unemployment rate is below 6%.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods of housing starts growth < 5% and unemployment rate > 6% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 5% decline in industrial production over the past month signals a weakening supply environment that supports negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days, provided the federal funds rate is above 2%.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods of industrial production decline < 3% and federal funds rate < 2% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 25-basis-point increase in the federal funds rate over the past 2 months signals a tightening monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.2% decline in the unemployment rate over the past quarter signals a strengthening labor market that supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days, provided the real GDP growth rate is above 2%.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods of unemployment rate decline < 0.1% and real GDP growth rate < 2% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
